questions.prompt: Which squares are the 'center' of the chessboard?
questions.explanation: The four center squares — d4, d5, e4, e5 — are the most important squares on the board. Pieces in the center control more squares and are harder to attack.

questions.prompt: Which of these is a good opening move?
options: e4 — controls the center and opens lines for the queen and bishop
a4 — a wing pawn move that doesn't fight for the center
h4 — same, just on the other wing
Na3 — a knight that goes to a less useful square
questions.explanation: e4 (and d4) are excellent opening moves because they fight for the center, free up the queen and king's bishop, and create immediate pressure. Wing pawn moves (a3/h3) and odd knight jumps (Na3/Nh3) waste tempo.

questions.prompt: Tap any of the four center squares — d4, d5, e4, or e5 — to identify where pieces want to be in the opening.
questions.explanation: d4 is one of the four center squares. So is d5, e4, e5. These are the squares your pieces want to influence in the opening — controlling them gives your army more power and flexibility.
